title: 帷幕
summary:
Judith and Poirot debate the necessity of marriage before Dr. Franklin enters the room. Dr. Franklin, a tall, thin, and absent-minded man, briefly discusses his work and his intention to read to his wife, Barbara, before departing. Judith expresses disdain for Barbara, criticizing her for obsessing over her illness and reading sensational novels. During the conversation, Poirot and Hastings discuss Hastings' preference for women with reddish-brown hair.

Hastings observes that Poirot’s longtime valet, George, has been replaced by a clumsy man named Curtis while George attends to his sick father. As Hastings retires to his room, he experiences a moment of doubt, wondering if Poirot’s claims of an impending murder at Styles Manor are merely the product of the detective's aging mind. However, Hastings ultimately decides to assume Poirot is correct and that the murderer, "X," is present in the house.

At dinner, Hastings is introduced to Miss Cole and Major Allerton. Hastings immediately dislikes Allerton, perceiving him as a handsome but dissolute rake who indulges in gambling and drinking. Despite the disapproval of Colonel Lestrade and Boyle Carrington, Allerton remains popular with the women, including Judith. As the guests sit for dinner, Hastings remains alert, attempting to identify which man in the house is the killer.
